chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-1775 of 2024 a/w C.P. Nos.D-1760 & 2410 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ PRESENT: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah HEARING (PRIORITY) CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.1734/2025. 2. For order on office objection. 3. For hearing of CMA No.8041/2024. 4. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 6th November 2025 Mr. Muhammad Ramzan, Advocate for Petitioners in C.P. No.D-1775 & 1760 of 2024. Mr. Ubedullah Malik, Advocate for Petitioner in C.P. No.D-2410/2024. Syed Fazal-ur-Rehman, Advocate for Respondent/Pakistan Steel Mills. Mr. Muhammad Hisham Mahar, Asst. A.G. Sindh. -*-*-*-*-*- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J: -- Petitioners are residents of Steel Town Colony Karachi and have filed these petitions praying that they have rented out shops there, but Pakistan Steel Mill is charging 100% excessive billing of electricity and has enhanced 100% rent of the shops. Learned counsel for respondent / Pakistan Steel Mills has placed on record a Decision of this Court dated 14.03.2025 passed in identical petitions (C.P. No.D-166 of 2025 and other connected matters). He has further stated that the petitioners may file a representation on the subject matter before Pakistan Steel Mills, which will be considered and decided in accordance with law. [Page 2] In view of above position, we dispose of these petitions, letting the petitioner(s) file appropriate representation(s) before Pakistan Steel Mill, which will decide the same within a period of two months.

JUDGE JUDGE *Farhan/PS*
